text:
$IONQ The military use case for quantum technology is becoming one of the most important investment themes in the sector.

Most investors focus on quantum computing. Governments and defense agencies are increasingly focused on quantum sensing, quantum networking, secure communications, and GPS-independent navigation.

According to recent analysis from the Center for Strategic and International Studies, quantum sensing has the potential to reshape modern warfare by enabling detection capabilities that conventional systems struggle to achieve. Applications include:

• Detecting submarines through quantum magnetometers
• Identifying underground tunnels and hidden infrastructure through quantum gravimeters
• GPS-denied navigation using atomic clocks and inertial sensors
• Enhanced intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance (ISR)
• Quantum-secure communications and networking
The strategic implication is simple:
Many of today's military advantages depend on remaining undetected.

If quantum sensing matures as expected, stealth aircraft, submarines, underground facilities, and even electronic warfare tactics could become more vulnerable to detection. CSIS compares the potential impact to the way radar transformed warfare in the 20th century.

This is where $IONQ becomes particularly interesting.
Most people still classify $IONQ as a pure-play quantum computing company. However, the company has spent the last two years building a broader quantum technology platform spanning:

• Quantum computing
• Quantum networking
• Quantum security
• Quantum sensing
• Government and defense applications

A major development was the acquisition of Vector Atomic, a company with more than $200 million in government contracts and technologies including:

• Atomic clocks
• Positioning, Navigation, and Timing (PNT) systems
• Gravimeters
• Inertial sensors

These technologies are directly applicable to defense, aerospace, and national security programs. Vector Atomic technology has also been associated with military and space applications, including programs supporting the U.S. Department of Defense.

$IONQ has also delivered quantum networking systems to the U.S. Air Force Research Laboratory and established IonQ Federal to serve U.S. government and allied defense customers. Importantly, $IONQ is not the only company positioned for this trend.
Other notable players include:
• $IBM — quantum computing and government research partnerships
• $QNT — quantum computing, cybersecurity, and defense-oriented research
• $RGTI — participant in U.S. government quantum initiatives 
• $QBTS — optimization and defense-related research applications
• $QUBT — photonics and sensing technologies
• Q-CTRL — quantum navigation systems designed for GPS-denied environments
• SandboxAQ — quantum sensing, cybersecurity, and government applications

The market may be underestimating a key point:
The first trillion-dollar quantum opportunity may not be quantum computing.

It may be the deployment of quantum sensing, timing, navigation, communications, and networking systems across defense, intelligence, aerospace, and critical infrastructure in sync with commercial use as well. The way I see it today, $IONQ seems to be the most structured company to date with the most resources, which is critical to deploy military related capabilities. 

If that thesis proves correct, $IONQ's expansion beyond computing and into sensing, networking, and federal programs could be one of the most significant strategic moves in the entire quantum industry. If you don't think Quantum will have military applications I recommend to look again...

text:
$GFS quietly continues to act well.

🤖 AI infrastructure, silicon photonics, co-packaged optics, and physical AI remain powerful tailwinds.

📈 After an 85% run, the stock is doing exactly what you’d want to see — consolidating above rising moving averages instead of giving back gains.

🎯 Recent shakeouts appear to be finding support near the 21MA, with price now attempting to reclaim the short-term trend.

👀 A move back through the recent highs could put the next leg higher in play.

Not many stocks can make a big move, consolidate, and still hold above the 10MA, 21MA, 50MA, and 200MA.

$GFS $NVDA $AVGO $MRVL $AMKR $TSM $LITE $CIEN 📈

text:
And here's the connection most investors haven't made yet:

The enabling technology for orbital infrastructure is photonics.

The same five-layer supply chain moving AI data at the speed of light inside terrestrial data centres is the same stack being positioned for space-based infrastructure.

text:
Memory and Photonics get most of the attention.

But there's a third constraint forming that I believe is still almost entirely under the radar.
The terrestrial grid.

AI data centres require 50–200 megawatts per facility. US grid interconnection queues now run five to ten years. There is no meaningful new power available at scale before 2030.

This is not speculation. It's a permitting and infrastructure timeline problem.

That’s the leg that gets repriced when token bills get reined in.

The AI trade is a whole infrastructure stack. 👇 https://t.co/gnRCD1yJui

text:
Let me let you guys in on a secret that can help you catch a lot of these moves before they happen so you're not stuck chasing and holding the bag.

Listen up.

Create a watchlist with the leaders in every sector that's been hot this year.

For example: Photonics, Semis, FAANG, Space, Robotics, Financials, Healthcare, Energy, etc.

Money is constantly rotating between these sectors. Your job is to determine where institutional money is positioned before each session.

Within those sector leaders, you'll have a group of smaller names that align with the same theme.

Something that really stood out to me last Friday was how memory names started bidding aggressively despite a weak tape. Those are some of my favorite setups because if the market decides to turn around, those names are usually the first to lead.

Between Thursday and Friday of last week, $SNDK ran from 1670 to 2000+.

While that name can be difficult for traders with smaller accounts to participate in, there are other stocks within the exact same theme that often make aggressive moves shortly after.

My focus immediately shifted to $WDC and $STX.

$WDC ran from 500 to 730 in just 3 sessions (+46%).

$STX ran from 870 to 1100 in just 3 sessions (+26%).

While many traders wait for 10 indicators to trigger before hitting the buy button and end up chasing these moves, there are other ways to get ahead.

The market leaves clues. You just have to know where to look.

Your job is to determine where institutional money is positioned before each session. Within those sector leaders, you'll have a group of smaller names that align with the same theme. Something that really stood out to me last Friday was how memory names started bidding aggressively despite a weak tape. Those are some of my favorite setups because if the market decides to turn around, those names are usually the first to lead. Between Thursday and Friday of last week, $SNDK ran from 1670 to 2000+. While that name can be difficult for traders with smaller accounts to participate in, there are other stocks within the exact same theme that often make aggressive moves shortly after. text:
AI infrastructure runs on more than compute. It runs on light.

Today in Sherman, Texas, Jensen Huang joined @CoherentCorp CEO Jim Anderson and local leaders to mark the expansion of U.S. manufacturing capacity for advanced photonics.

The technology is behind the lasers and optical links that move data across modern AI systems, strengthening the American manufacturing base behind the AI era.

text:
🚨 SCIENTISTS JUST ACHIEVED OPTICAL MULTISTABILITY IN A TINY MICROCAVITY  A BIG STEP FOR PHOTONIC MEMORY.

In a new paper published in Nature Nanotechnology, researchers demonstrated multiple stable optical states inside a compact microcavity by using near-exceptional coupling. 

This allows the device to “remember” different light intensities as distinct, stable states  something normally only possible in much larger or more complex systems.
Optical multistability is a key requirement for multilevel optical memory and all-optical computing. Until now, achieving it in a small enough footprint for on-chip use has been extremely difficult because optical nonlinearities are usually very weak.

Why this matters:

• It brings us closer to practical on-chip optical memory that can store multiple bits of information per device
• Exceptional-point physics can dramatically enhance effective nonlinearity without needing exotic materials
• Compact multistable devices could enable faster, more energy-efficient photonic processors
• It shows how non-Hermitian physics is becoming a powerful design tool in nanophotonics

The deeper implication:

We’re learning to engineer light itself to behave in increasingly complex and useful ways inside tiny structures. By operating near exceptional points, researchers can coax weak optical nonlinearities into producing strong, multistable behavior.

 This kind of advance moves photonic computing from theory toward something that can actually be fabricated at scale on a chip.

If this approach can be further optimized and integrated with existing silicon photonics, it could become a building block for future light-based memory and logic.

How do you think compact optical multistability could accelerate the development of photonic computers?

Follow for more frontier nanophotonics, exceptional-point physics, and optical computing.

Nokia in Allentown, PA — 10x capacity for photonics packaging.

Hottest Sectors off the June 5th bloodbath 🩸

I know it's just been a few days but never too early to see which sectors are jumping out.

So here's the list. 7 trading days data only.

+23% Memory/Storage $SNDK $MU
+20% Payments $DAVE $SEZL
+14%  Neo-clouds $NBIS $IREN $CRWV
+14%    Networking $ALAB $CRDO
+13%  My Current Portfolio
+8%    Semis $SMH
+7%    My Older Portfolio from March 30 lows
+4%    Energy/Power: $BE $GEV $VRT
+2%    S&P 500 $SPX
0%      Photonics/Optics $LITE $AAOI $AXTI
0%      Compute $NVDA $AVGO
-3%     Software $APP $RDDT
-9%    Defense $ONDS $KRKNF

So what's the insight?

Memory, Payments, Networking and Noe-clouds off to a blazing start. Payments is new in this move up. Early signs these could be the leaders of the next leg up.

I am happy with the portfolio re-shuffle. As you can see, my current portfolio is already pulling away from the one I held during the initial push off from March 30 lows. 

My bet is power and photonics will catch up in the next market grind higher. All these stocks have been taking a breather.

text:
ChatGPT:『まず、この記事の「シリコンバレーの企業」を単なる企業名の列挙として理解するより、
「AIデータセンター陣営」対「通信キャリア陣営」
の構図として読む方が本質的です。
現在の光技術競争は、
NTT → 通信網全体を光化する IOWN/APN
NVIDIA・Broadcom → AIクラスタ内部を光化する CPO（Co-Packaged Optics）
Meta・Microsoft・Google → 超巨大データセンターの内部配線を光化
という三層構造になっています。�
リコーブログ、ほかに 1 件
特に重要なのは、
「AIと通信は融合する」
というシリコンバレー側の発言です。
これは表面的にはNTTに好意的に見えますが、実際には
「通信もAIインフラの一部になる」
という意味である可能性があります。
つまり、
NTTの発想 　→ 通信ネットワークが中心 　→ AIはその上で動く
NVIDIAの発想 　→ AIクラスタが中心 　→ 通信はAIクラスタを繋ぐ手段
です。
ここに構造的な対立があります。
実際、現在の光電融合技術の主戦場は、
シリコンフォトニクス
光インターコネクト
CPO
光スイッチ
へ移行しており、
NTT自身もIOWNの中核技術としてシリコンフォトニクスや光電融合デバイス（PEC）を推進しています。�
NTT、ほかに 1 件
さらに興味深いのは、
記事が「米半導体の巨人」と表現している点です。
通信企業ではなく、
NVIDIA
Broadcom
が主語になっている。
これは技術主導権が
通信業界 → 半導体業界
へ移ったことを意味します。�
リコーブログ
私ならこの記事の核心を一文でこう要約します。
「IOWNが危機なのではなく、AI時代には通信が主役ではなくなりつつある。」
ということです。
さらに長期的には、
NTTが目指す
APN（All Photonics Network）
6G
デジタルツイン
の世界観と、
NVIDIAが進める
AI Factory
光AIクラスタ
OCI（Optical Compute Interconnect）
の世界観は、
実はかなり収束しています。
したがって本当の問題は、
「IOWNが負けるか」
ではなく、
「IOWNがNVIDIA陣営の標準に吸収されるか、それとも標準そのものを握れるか」
です。
現時点（2026年）では、残念ながら標準化主導権はNVIDIA・Broadcom側へ傾き始めていると見るのが自然でしょう。NTTは技術的には先行部分があるものの、AI需要を直接握っているのは通信キャリアではなくAIインフラ企業だからです。�

𝗙𝗼𝗰𝘂𝘀 𝗧𝗵𝗲𝗺𝗲𝘀
Agentic AI

The AI trade continues its rally. After a brief pullback money is rushing back into the various pockets: Memory, Photonics, Chip pick and shovels, and power. With another gap up the tactical theme of the year is to buy pullbacks for the best risk/reward and to increase the probability of success. Up days are large and gaps are followed by sharp shallow pullback that are prone to knock out the week hands. Buying RS on weakness is the name of the game.

𝗠𝗮𝗿𝗸𝗲𝘁 𝗖𝗼𝗺𝗺𝗲𝗻𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘆
Big gap up in the indices. 3% on QQQ, almost 2% SPY.

Oil, 10Y, down. Dollar stable/pulling back. Big bounce in precious metals and crypto though gold/silver/BTC/ETH are still broken charts.

𝗣𝗲𝗿𝘀𝗼𝗻𝗮𝗹 𝗖𝗼𝗺𝗺𝗲𝗻𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘆
My process is improving, I'm becoming more efficient, I'm seeing more opportunities, I'm spending more time creating model books. Continuing to work towards my goal of mastery. The equity curve doesn't show it yet but I'm seeing personal progress that is incredibly motivating.

text:
ReRating Legacy Companies. $AKAM $AMKR $MCHP $STM $KLIC

Everyone right now is looking at small caps with high risk high reward kinda story but there is an underlying love story going on in the markets right now. Re rating legacy companies turning into AI infra, advanced packaging companies. 

$AKAM - from legacy CDN Company to Edge AI Infra, Cyber Security, Inference Cloud

$AMKR - A low multiple OSAT Consumer Electronics Cyclical company to Advanced Packaging AI bottleneck Company

$MCHP - Industrial/Auto MCU company to Data center Connectivity (PCIe, power management and data center products) 

$STM - Auto/Industrial Semi Company to AI Data Center supplier (optical connectivity, power and thermal management and SiC photonics

$KLIC - wire bonding company to Advanced Packaging AI Bottleneck (Thermo compression bonding, HBM memory packaging, Chiplets, CPO)

All of the companies moved 80-100% in last 2 months. Not so crowded and not so talked about names. Might balance portfolio risk if anyone looking for similar returns but with a mature company. 

80% of the high flyers right now moved similar amounts (+/-15%) in last 2 months too.

And it’s compelling. The company is sampling quad 100G, quad 200G, and 32x50G NRZ uVCSELs next quarter, with high-volume ramp targeted for early 2027. The 200G parts clock in at 37GHz bandwidth, a meaningful threshold for 200G-per-lane operation. Importantly, PicoJool is building on one of the largest existing optical manufacturing ecosystems. The company’s thesis is that GaAs VCSEL capacity scales far more easily than the InP laser supply chains that many investors have focused on. PicoJool is building on GaAs with WIN Semiconductor, which has shipped over a billion VCSELs for 3D sensing applications. This decision to stay on GaAs instead of chasing indium phosphide or silicon photonics is important. GaAs VCSEL manufacturing capacity is vastly larger than merchant InP laser capacity. I'm also interested in the "slow and wide" option. The 32x50G uVCSEL isn't trying to push a single lane to extreme speeds. It runs 32 lanes at 50G NRZ, a massively parallel approach that fits naturally into co-packaged optics and near-packaged optics architectures. The cost target is to compete with copper which makes sense for AI scale-up interconnect, where you're wiring hundreds of GPUs inside a rack. The founder has done this before, too. Al Yuen shipped the first GbE VCSELs in 1996. At Alvesta, his first startup, he invented the active optical cable in 2001, the now-standard format where the E/O conversion lives inside the cable assembly rather than a separate pluggable module. Alvesta was acquired by Emcore in 2002. He went on to develop vertical oxidation techniques for high-volume VCSEL fabrication in 2016. Roadmap goes to 3.2T. 他们确认了年报和零分红，他们通过2025财年的损益表和资产负债表,并明确决议不向2025财年分配任何股息，再投资增长的逻辑确认。董事会与CEO获免责。 2. 他们确立了董事会改组，连任Bami Bastani会继续任董事长、Karin Raj、Todd Thomson;并且他们新增两位董事Joakim Nideborn担任副董事长)和Helena Svancar，这正是我们之前关注的AGM董事会变动。 董事薪酬方案包括年度津贴+股票持有计划：每人100万瑞典克朗,约一半用于购买Sivers股票并锁定1年，这一点能说明现有董事对于公司的长期看好！ 另外董事长薪酬105万克朗、副董事长60万克朗、其他董事各35万克朗,各委员会主席/成员另有额度。董事长将获得美国医疗保险覆盖这从侧面印证公司国际化和赴美布局。 这第二点我们完全可以看出来公司对于 $sive股价的决心和公司转变为美国企业的布局进程，美国医疗保险只会覆盖给长期留在美国的人，换句话说你想留在美国你才会去买美国医疗保险。 他们用自己的一半薪酬去购买 $sive这一点真的很值得敬佩！ 3. 他们的审计师Deloitte连任。 4. 按瑞典公司法批准董事会薪酬报告。 5. 他们增发授权这里15%稀释上限，这也是这一次的核心议案，授权董事会在下届AGM前,可一次或多次发行不超过5384万9956股普通股我们换算后对应稀释后约15%股本的新股/认股权证/可转债,可不按比例分配给现有股东,价格按发行时市场价，这一段比较重要，没有低价稀释。 这一条的重点在于：这是一个”备用授权”,目前没有具体执行计划,不代表立即稀释，这意味着目前没有任何稀释压力，并且就算要稀释我个人判断也是在股价上涨时。 6.在可转债方面，327k可转债是确认无误。 批准3月3日董事会决议,通过Bootstrap Europe 4.0认购622,719份面值4.77瑞典克朗的可转债,合计32.7万美元,年利率10.85%,2029年底到期(若未提前转股)。 这次AGM基本都是”走完法律流程”性质的确认,核心议案此前都已预告,市场大概率已price in。没有出现否决案、审计师更换、或临时新增的负面议案,本身就是一个”排雷成功”的信号，尤其考虑到此前空头报告、内幕交易调查等舆论压力下,股东大会顺利通过所有议案,本身对短期情绪是个利好。所以我们没有听到什么超预期的消息也没有听到纳斯达克的任何消息是很正常的。 很棒，在现在外面全是谣言和诽谤的情况下，这些决议都顺利通过了！ 非常好！特别是零分红+再投资逻辑获正式确认,符合我们对于”成长期公司应该把现金投入CPO/InP产能扩张”的判断框架。 #SIVE #SiversSemiconductors #AGM #股东大会 #CPO #硅光 #InP激光器 #GlobalFoundries #SCALE #AI infrastructure #光通信 🔔 The Daily Semi-Cap — June 15, 2026

📊 SMH: $647.10 (+4.38%)

📈 Earnings:
• No new semiconductor earnings released today.

🔥 Major Developments:
• Qualcomm has been in talks to acquire Tenstorrent, with a discussed price around $8-10B. The read-through is straightforward: Qualcomm wants more AI-chip architecture talent and another shot at pushing beyond smartphones into custom compute.
• PicoJool announced 200G VCSELs and MicroVCSELs for AI scale-up links, with quad 100G/200G and 32x50G parts sampling next quarter and high-volume ramp targeted for early 2027. The interesting angle is GaAs scale: a mature VCSEL supply chain trying to compete with copper for dense short-reach GPU interconnects.
• IQE signed a multi-year InP epiwafer supply agreement with Tower Semiconductor tied to Tower’s silicon photonics platform. The agreement covers 200G/lane pluggables, next-gen 400G/lane modulators, and optical-circuit-switch applications, adding another data point that AI optical capacity is getting locked up years ahead.
• Semis ripped as the AI-infrastructure tape went full risk-on: SMH closed +4.38%, with memory and accelerator-adjacent names leading. The move keeps confirming that investors are still treating AI hardware, HBM, custom silicon, and optical networking as the cleanest places to be when macro pressure eases.

text:
$IQE deep dive:

FY2025 revenue was about GBP 97M, down 17.6% YoY. Photonics was GBP 57.1M, up 15%, while wireless was GBP 40.1M, down 40%. The latest yfinance snapshot had GBP 97.3M TTM revenue, 1.39% gross margin, negative EBITDA around GBP 8.2M, GBP 15.7M cash and GBP 91.7M debt.

The thesis is upstream photonics scarcity. IQE makes compound-semiconductor epiwafers/materials, including InP/GaN/GaAs, not the sexy final optical module. If AI data centers keep pushing 800G/1.6T optics and future co-packaged optics, the chokepoint can move upstream to hard-to-replicate wafer and epitaxy capacity.

The new catalyst is Tower Semiconductor. IQE signed a multi-year InP epiwafer supply agreement for AI-driven optical connectivity, including Tower's 200Gb/s-per-lane pluggable platform, next-gen 400Gb/s-per-lane modulator prototyping, and optical-circuit-switch work. It includes a year-one minimum purchase commitment and later minimum volume commitments. That is way better than "trust me bro, AI optics."

Second catalyst: MACOM's strategic investment and long-term supply agreements tied to the GBP 81M fundraising. That cleaned up some balance-sheet stress and validated the capacity. Third: the old consensus path was only about GBP 117M FY2026 revenue and GBP 140M FY2027 revenue, so any real AI/datacenter mix disclosure or guide raise would matter.

Risks are not cute. The stock has already ripped from distressed levels to 57.71GBX. Fully diluted market cap is roughly GBP 750M on sub-GBP 100M revenue and barely-there margins. New shares/convertibles diluted holders. HSBC covenant waiver history says the old balance sheet was fragile. And strategic customers can secure supply without giving IQE huge pricing power.

Entry/Verdict: CATALYST WATCH / optional tiny scout only, not a normal buy. I would not add size here after the vertical rerate. Clean entry is a pullback toward/under 30p with the thesis intact, or paying up only after Tower/MACOM revenue, backlog, guide, gross margin, or estimate revisions actually move.

This is real proof, not just photonics basket noise. But the market is already asking IQE to become strategic scarcity, not a cyclical wafer supplier with a cool press release.

We'll see how it goes.

text:
The biggest sectors in the market five years from now will be these. Position early and let the decade do the work.

AI Infrastructure. The backbone everything runs on. The data centers, the compute, the owned infrastructure powering every model on earth. Every other sector on this list runs on top of it.

Space. Connectivity and infrastructure moving off the planet and into orbit. Direct to phone networks, launch, and satellite constellations turning space into the next commercial frontier.

Photonics. The plumbing that moves the data. As clusters scale to hundreds of thousands of GPUs, moving information at the speed of light becomes the constraint. A market tripling this decade as 800G goes to 1.6T goes to 3.2T.

Nuclear Energy. The only power source that can actually feed AI at scale. Data centers need enormous, constant, carbon free baseload, and the grid cannot deliver it fast enough. Nuclear is the answer the entire industry is racing toward.

Memory. The bottleneck nobody can build fast enough. Every AI server needs 8 to 10 times the memory of a normal one, the makers are sold out, and the shortage runs for years. No memory, no AI.

Humanoid Robotics. AI stepping out of the screen and into the physical world. A market going from under $2 billion today to $38 billion by 2035. The companies making the brains, eyes, and sensors win no matter which robot wins.

Quantum Computing. The frontier that breaks the limits of classical computing entirely. Still early, still small, but governments and the biggest tech companies on earth are pouring billions in. The most asymmetric bet of the group.

Seven sectors. One direction. This is where the wealth of the next decade gets created, and we are early in every single one.

text:
The controversy surrounding computational photonics reminds me of the turn of the Century when high definition video products were released and marketed as "the 35mm film killer" 

Save a ton on production/post by shooting and finishing on high definition video instead of film

text:
You're reading about optics. You dive deep into photonics. You're wondering wth is Gan-on-Sci...

But you're missing one of the biggest chokepoints of all.

At sub-5nm, silicon dioxide fails. Electrons tunnel through it. The gate leaks. You need hafnium oxide — deposited atom by atom, precursor chemistry doing work no photolithography machine can substitute.

That's the chokepoint. And $4369.T (Tri Chemical Laboratories) owns the master patents.

Two challenges filed by Merck — one of the world's largest specialty chemical companies — to invalidate those patents. Both defeated. The moat isn't asserted. It survived industrial warfare.

Samsung recently confirmed a sourcing shift toward SK trichem, a JV where Tri Chemical holds 35% equity and the underlying IP. That's the thesis crystallizing in real time — not through a press release, but through actual procurement decisions.

Q1 FY2026: operating profit ¥2.07B, up 20.8% YoY. H1 FY2026 printed record-high net sales. The Minami-Alps facility is now in build-out — expanding into next-gen etching materials while hafnium volume ramps for 2nm production.

Here's why nobody's watching: $4369.T trades on the Tokyo exchange with essentially zero English-language institutional coverage. Western fintwit has no awareness of this name.

Price: ~¥3,980. 52-week range ¥2,455–4,455. Currently 11% off ATH, consolidating above prior highs. Not parabolic. Clean structure.

The 2nm ramp is 12–18 months from meaningful production volume. The precursor chemistry gets designed in first — that's already happening. By the time the node-shrink narrative reaches Western retail, the supply contracts are signed.

Find the bottleneck before the narrative does.

text:
Tower Semiconductor and Marvell Ship Over Five Million Coherent Photonic ICs
> Tower has collaborated with Marvell to advance next-generation coherent technologies, including process capabilities, such as the integration of non-silicon materials, the 3D integration of electronics and the enablement of advanced optical packaging such as V-Grooves, which serve to extend the performance and functionality of silicon photonics platforms.
> Coherent PICs are complex because they must control the phase and polarization of light, not just the amplitude. The design and process requirements for these PICs are far more stringent compared to simpler direct-detect chips.

text:
2026-06-18 半导体供应链瓶颈每日观察

核心判断：今天没有改变半导体供应链瓶颈大排序的强证据，但 AMKR/美国本土先进封装在社区里升温，值得进入明日验证清单；强证据层仍由 HBM、CoWoS/SoIC/先进封装设备和 InP epiwafer 线索支撑。

1. 今日最重要强证据仍不是新公告，而是对已有硬证据的延续复核。TSMC 月营收继续支持 AI 先进制程和先进封装景气，但还不能直接拆成 CoWoS/SoIC 产能新增；IQE/Tower 多年 InP epiwafer 协议仍是本周 CPO/硅光上游材料最强官方信号；NVIDIA Vera Rubin 与 Spectrum-X Ethernet Photonics 继续支持硅光方向；Micron HBM4 和 ASMPT C2W TCB repeat order 分别支撑 HBM 与先进封装设备层。

2. 对瓶颈层级排序的影响：大排序不变。第一梯队仍是 HBM/先进 DRAM、CoWoS/SoIC/2.5D/3D 先进封装、先进封装设备；CPO/硅光/光纤保持第二梯队上沿；InP epiwafer / InP laser / 硅光上游材料维持上调后的高跟踪优先级；glass substrate、panel-level packaging、CoPoS 和高端基板材料仍是中长期线索，暂不提前当成 2026 业绩弹性。

3. 标的跟踪权重变化：TSMC、HBM 三家、ASMPT/BESI/DISCO/测试设备这条主线不下调。IQE、Tower、Coherent、Lumentum、GF、Broadcom/Marvell 光链继续加权，但等待金额、volume、客户和产能数据。AMKR 小幅上调到验证清单，原因是社区重新把它看作美国 advanced packaging/test 基础设施资产，而不仅是传统 OSAT；但目前只有 Arizona campus 和与 TSMC 合作叙事，缺客户、订单、产能爬坡、收入拆分和经济性。

4. 社区观点是否改变判断：只改变细分跟踪优先级，不改变一级排序。AMKR 是今天最值得跟的新社区线索，但证据强度仍是 Weak/Medium；Nokia advanced packaging 仍未被官方材料确认，不能升级；SOCAMM vs HBM4 的分歧继续作为反方验证问题，不能把 CPU-side LPDDR5X/SOCAMM 配置变化直接读成 GPU-side HBM4 需求逆转。

5. 下一步验证信号：第一，查 AMKR Arizona 与 TSMC/客户的真实绑定、产能时间表、capex、先进封装收入和现金流回报。第二，继续拆 IQE/Tower 协议的最低采购、volume、定价、Tower 客户、IQE 产能和毛利。第三，等 TSMC 6 月营收和 Q2 法说会，看 CoWoS/SoIC/3DFabric 是否有更清晰拆分。第四，验证 Micron/SK hynix/Samsung 的 HBM4 份额、良率、ASP、capacity sold-out 和客户长约。第五，继续查 glass substrate/CoPoS 的量产时间、良率、成本和 2026 订单弹性。

三家、ASMPT/BESI/DISCO/测试设备这条主线不下调。IQE、Tower、Coherent、Lumentum、GF、Broadcom/Marvell 光链继续加权，但等待金额、volume、客户和产能数据。AMKR 小幅上调到验证清单，原因是社区重新把它看作美国 advanced packaging/test 基础设施资产，而不仅是传统 OSAT；但目前只有 Arizona campus 和与 TSMC 合作叙事，缺客户、订单、产能爬坡、收入拆分和经济性。 4. 社区观点是否改变判断：只改变细分跟踪优先级，不改变一级排序。AMKR 是今天最值得跟的新社区线索，但证据强度仍是 Weak/Medium；Nokia advanced packaging 仍未被官方材料确认，不能升级；SOCAMM vs HBM4 的分歧继续作为反方验证问题，不能把 CPU-side LPDDR5X/SOCAMM 配置变化直接读成 GPU-side HBM4 需求逆转。 5. 下一步验证信号：第一，查 AMKR Arizona 与 TSMC/客户的真实绑定、产能时间表、capex、先进封装收入和现金流回报。第二，继续拆 IQE/Tower 协议的最低采购、volume、定价、Tower 客户、IQE 产能和毛利。第三，等 TSMC 6 月营收和 Q2 法说会，看 CoWoS/SoIC/3DFabric 是否有更清晰拆分。第四，验证 Micron/SK hynix/Samsung 的 HBM4 份额、良率、ASP、capacity sold-out 和客户长约。第五，继续查 glass substrate/CoPoS 的量产时间、良率、成本和 2026 订单弹性。"}}}, "rest_id": "2067727745514397989", text:
The generalists completely misjudged $TRT looking at it through the lens of a legacy semiconductor testing company.

They missed the absolute mechanical reality that you cannot scale high-performance computing without cross-stack reliability testing, and the data layer shows all four pillars are now fully running.
The thesis hitting the tape proves exactly why the runway toward a $1B valuation has so much structural momentum:

Memory & Compute Convergence: Their back-end semiconductor solutions segment just hit a massive 124% YoY explosion. Securing multi-million dollar follow-on orders for specialized burn-in boards backstopping next-generation AI GPU platforms proves they are an un-bypassable gatekeeper for the core compute and HBM stack.  

The Power & Photonics Layer: The crowd is totally blind to their dynamic tester system shipments for Silicon Carbide (SiC) and Gallium Nitride (GaN) power modules. As advanced hardware environments transition from copper to light, the structural heat and signal redlining mean automated power testing and optical co-packaging reliability are mandatory toll-booths, not optional choices.  

They are aggressively expanding their physical footprint with a massive new 104,000-square-foot facility in Malaysia just to absorb the sheer velocity of AI hardware testing demand.  

The tourists are still trying to figure out if the momentum is real, while the smart money is just watching the fundamental checklist get ticked off one by one. Still incredibly early in the infrastructure cycle.  

Long the cross-stack bottlenecks.

text:
The market cap disparity here is where the absolute percentage torque is hiding.

While generalists chase the massive liquidity pool of a larger cap play like $AAOI, they completely miss the structural math of relative scaling. Owning both is the ultimate play on the broader optical super-cycle, but ignoring the sheer headroom on Sivers ($SIVE) at these valuation levels is a massive blind spot.

The structural reality between the two tells you exactly where the asymmetric upside sits:

The Valuation Ceiling: $AAOI is an exceptional, vertically integrated photonics asset, but a larger market cap means moving the needle 2x or 3x takes an astronomical amount of capital. $SIVE sitting at a fraction of that size means any major US hyperscaler contract win translates into immediate, hyper-violent torque on the stock price.

The Transatlantic Re-Rate: The market continues to discount Sivers because of its European listing heritage, failing to see the structural pipeline of its ongoing US flip. As it completes the transition into a fully US-domiciled entity, it systematically opens the floodgates for domestic small-cap growth funds that are contractually prohibited from buying foreign listings.
 
The 100 SEK Threshold: Complaining about a past 2,000% run is classic rear-view mirror trading. Crossing the 100 SEK ($10) mark didn't signal the end of the move, it cleared the institutional liquidity mandates that kept the largest desks from accumulating the float.

You don't have to pick a single winner when the entire copper-to-light paradigm shift is choking out global capacity. But if you are hunting for raw, high-torque percentage expansion as the independent Western chokepoint gets priced in, the path of least resistance is incredibly clear.

Long both, but $AAOI is my personal favorite ! 📈

Traditional electronics move data using electricity through copper wires. Photonics moves data using light through fiber-optic cables. Light can carry much more information and travel much farther than electrical signals. ⸻ What is a transceiver? A transceiver is a small device that sits at each end of a fiber-optic cable. Its job is to: Take electrical data from a server or switch. -Convert it into flashes of light. -Send that light through fiber. -Receive light coming back. -Convert it back into electrical data. Think of it as a translator between electronics and light. ⸻ Where does $AAOI fit in? $AAOI (Applied Optoelectronics) manufactures: -Optical transceivers -Lasers -Fiber-optic networking components Their products are used inside: AI data centers Cloud providers Telecom networks Broadband networks ⸻ Why AI is making this important A modern AI data center may contain: Thousands of GPUs Massive amounts of data moving between them The GPUs can’t work together efficiently unless they can communicate extremely fast. That’s where optical networking comes in. The data path looks like: GPU → Switch → Transceiver → Fiber → Transceiver → Switch → GPU Every connection requires optical hardware."}}}, "previous_counts": {"bookmark_count": 0, "favorite_count": 0, "quote_count": 0, "reply_count": 0, "retweet_count": 0}, "rest_id": "2067444958332584107", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "34614", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2068110479390855576", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "7999", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
🔔 The Daily Semi-Cap — June 19, 2026

📊 SMH: $659.88 (+5.76%)

📈 Earnings:
• No new semiconductor earnings released today.

🔥 Major Developments:
• China’s AI-infrastructure roadmap keeps broadening: a proposed ~$295B five-year data-center buildout is pulling domestic optics, InP substrates, EMLs, CW lasers, silicon photonics, and optical engines deeper into the strategic supply chain.
• Rubin Ultra’s memory stack debate shifted toward 12-hi HBM near term rather than 16-hi, with hybrid-bonding cost/yield still looking too painful for broad HBM insertion before later generations.
• Architect Labs raised $24M for custom chip development, another sign that venture dollars are still hunting for AI silicon alternatives beyond the GPU incumbents.
• John Jumper, the Nobel-winning AlphaFold leader, left Google DeepMind for Anthropic, extending the frontier-lab talent war that ultimately feeds the compute, networking, and power arms race.

text:
Regarding $SIVE vs $AAOI

$AAOI is in photonics and thus well positioned, but it has baggage in its history and 13b market cap.

$SIVEV is in photonics and has a clean history and a 2.8b market cap.

It's easy to see Sivers stands to gain a lot more in percentage terms.

Yes, you can argue American companies grow faster than European, but Sivers is well underway to becoming an American company.

The only people shilling $AAOI and not $SIVE are the people who are holding $AAOI and missed $SIVE's 2000% run and are mad about it.

But if you're thinking of buying either right now $SIVE is the easy choice.

text:
Portfolio Update (Mid-June'26): +161% (YTD)

1. $SIVE (37%) - InP laser supply is not enough to fill demand. Tied to all hyperscaler supply chains & OEMs. 

$JBL, Ayar Labs & $GFS being the most notable confirmation of volume production from H2'26. 

Holding this intact. I'm looking for my next entry now. Said I would keep adding on nice entries until year end. 

Haven't trimmed a single share because the story hasn't even played out yet. We're still early. Long $SIVE! 

2. $NBIS (26.5%) - 4th hyperscaler in the making. Definitely a great long. AI compute demand keeps growing. 

Software moat makes them #1. That's where applications run, at the end of the day. 

Trimmed from 35% to 25%. $400 looks fair by year end but feel like we will have a little slow down. 

Could be wrong too. So wouldn't recommend for longs that don't want to rotate. Tax cuts are also a thing to consider. Long $NBIS! 

3. Cash (14%) - Built a cash position to strategically enter into a new possible huge long position or maybe a couple of small longer positions. Possibly add into my existing ones like $BRUN, $TRT, etc. 

While I did say I was happy with memory exposure, still seems low. Will try to gain more exposure over there. 

4. $BRUN (3.5%) - NVDA exemplar cloud certification is a sign of strong reliability. Why would $DELL CEO just mention them like that? Looks to be the next $CRWV. 

Their revenue growth and projections keep scaling at a level that's consistent and unbelievably good. 

Feel like 2027 will be the year for them. We got some slight short-term share lockup stuff hovering that could provide opportunities to buy but also keep the stock out of radar of the big guns. Long $BRUN!

5. $PENG (6%) - AI server demand growth is so high. I got surprised when $DELL released their earnings. 

$PENG having stronger long-term upside with photonic & memory exposure that could expand margins. Long $PENG! 

6. $TRT (4.5%) - Backend testing and sales of burn-in-boards for testing chips. Growing at triple-digits and a very good balance sheet. 

10x potential as chip's margin for error before release is lower. Demand > supply gap being one of the main reasons. Long $TRT! 

7. Swings & Longs combined (8.5%) - $AAOI, $CRDO, $SMCI, $KEEL

$AAOI, $CRDO are swings that I am looking to keep a few as longs due to huge growth in the coming years. Still feels really early. Photonics story in 2027 is going to be even bigger.

$SMCI & $KEEL are definitely the ones that I won't hold long-term. 

$KEEL will go out when leases confirm with hyperscalers hit on the news and it reaches somewhere around $10+. Maybe $20? Let us see! 

$SMCI is being criticized for the right reasons but I feel they can have a great turnaround and margins will get better. 4x from here. Not the quickest swing. Cheap! 0.5x sales bro. 

One thing I didn't mention earlier was $SMCI was a swing position and not a long position. So I added it to my swing list now. 

Also sold $ASTS since I wanted to fill my cash position again. But feels like a $100 soon. Don't know why street is being irrational here after Bluebird successful launch.

$MU is also a small position in this to ride to $2T+. I want to increase memory exposure, as I mentioned. So I'll be doing that once I can.

text:
This is the right way to look at the AI infrastructure trade.

Nvidia is not “dead” because ASICs are coming.

But the money flow is widening.

Training made GPUs the obvious winner. Inference at massive scale creates room for custom chips, edge AI, photonics, memory, power, and TSMC to matter even more.

The next winners may not replace Nvidia.

They may grow around it.

text:
土曜朝ニュース（AI光接続）

AIデータセンターの主戦場がGPUだけじゃなく「つなぐ部分」に移ってきた。

銅配線は短距離なら強い。でもラックが巨大化すると電力・発熱・遅延がボトルネックになる。

$NVDA はCPO/シリコンフォトニクスで従来の着脱式光トランシーバ比5倍の電力効率をうたう。Spectrum-X Ethernet Photonicsは409.6Tb/sで2026年後半提供予定。

TowerはNVIDIA向け1.6T光モジュールで協業。MarketsandMarketsはSiPho市場を2024年$2.16Bから2030年$9.65Bへ拡大と予測。

GPUの次は「電力」と「光」。

見ておきたいのは
$LITE  $COHR  $CRDO  $AVGO  $MRVL  $ANET  

レーザー・光モジュール・高速接続がAIインフラの次の争奪戦になってきた。

text:
$AAOI photonics is an AI data-center throughput test.

The important point is not that fiber is faster than copper in the abstract. It is that GPU clusters only become useful when data can move between servers, switches, and accelerators without turning networking into the bottleneck.

That makes optical transceivers a hidden scaling layer. More GPUs create more east-west traffic, more switch bandwidth, and more demand for components that convert electrical signals into light and back again.

Watch data-center orders, transceiver mix, gross margin, customer concentration, and whether AI cluster builds keep lifting optical networking spend after the first hardware excitement fades.

text:
Copper wire has run the world for over a century. The era that replaces it is starting right now, and it might be the most important infrastructure shift of the decade.

Here is the simple version of what is happening.

Inside an AI data center, thousands of chips have to talk to each other constantly. For a hundred years machines did this the same way, by sending electricity down copper wires. That always worked. Until AI.

 The chips now move so much data so fast that copper physically cannot keep up. Push electricity that hard down a wire and it turns into heat, the signal falls apart, and it can barely cross a single rack before it dies.

So the entire industry is ripping out the copper and replacing it with light. Instead of electricity down a wire, data travels as pulses of light through glass fiber. Light runs cooler, travels farther, and carries vastly more information. 

In a world where power and heat are the limits on how much AI you can build, switching to light is not a preference. It is the only way forward.

What makes this generational rather than just another upgrade is the staircase the whole industry is now climbing. 

It went from 400G to 800G, the workhorse today, already shipping in the tens of millions. 

Now it is climbing to 1.6T, double the bandwidth in the same space, with NVIDIA's newest systems needing over 160 of these per single rack. 

Next comes 3.2T, doubling it again. 

And what used to be a five year upgrade cycle has collapsed to one or two years. These generations are not replacing each other one at a time. 800G, 1.6T, and 3.2T are ramping on top of one another all at once.

Now the size of the prize. The optical transceiver market sat around $15 billion in 2025 and is heading toward $36 billion by 2033, with some forecasts for the AI portion alone reaching far higher. 

The silicon photonics piece inside it grows from under $1 billion to over $6 billion by 2030. This is a market doubling and then doubling again, driven by a buildout that has barely started.

What does it actually mean? It means every company that makes the lasers, the chips, and the modules that turn electricity into light gets paid on every step up that staircase, for years.

$AAOI makes its own lasers in-house, the exact bottleneck part the whole industry is fighting over. 

$CRDO makes the chips that keep these blazing fast connections clean and reliable. 

$LITE and $COHR are the photonics giants supplying the lasers and transceivers everyone depends on. 

$MRVL and $AVGO build the custom silicon at the center of it all.

Copper carried the last hundred years. Light carries the next hundred. And the wiring is being torn out and replaced right now.

The era that replaces it is starting right now, and it might be the most important infrastructure shift of the decade. Here is the simple version of what is happening. Inside an AI data center, thousands of chips have to talk to each other constantly. For a hundred years machines did this the same way, by sending electricity down copper wires. That always worked. Until AI. The chips now move so much data so fast that copper physically cannot keep up. Push electricity that hard down a wire and it turns into heat, the signal falls apart, and it can barely cross a single rack before it dies. So the entire industry is ripping out the copper and replacing it with light. Instead of electricity down a wire, data travels as pulses of light through glass fiber. Light runs cooler, travels farther, and carries vastly more information. In a world where power and heat are the limits on how much AI you can build, switching to light is not a preference. It is the only way forward. What makes this generational rather than just another upgrade is the staircase the whole industry is now climbing. It went from 400G to 800G, the workhorse today, already shipping in the tens of millions. Now it is climbing to 1.6T, double the bandwidth in the same space, with NVIDIA's newest systems needing over 160 of these per single rack. Next comes 3.2T, doubling it again. And what used to be a five year upgrade cycle has collapsed to one or two years. These generations are not replacing each other one at a time. 800G, 1.6T, and 3.2T are ramping on top of one another all at once. Now the size of the prize. The optical transceiver market sat around $15 billion in 2025 and is heading toward $36 billion by 2033, with some forecasts for the AI portion alone reaching far higher. The silicon photonics piece inside it grows from under $1 billion to over $6 billion by 2030. This is a market doubling and then doubling again, driven by a buildout that has barely started. What does it actually mean? It means every company that makes the lasers, the chips, and the modules that turn electricity into light gets paid on every step up that staircase, for years. $AAOI makes its own lasers in-house, the exact bottleneck part the whole industry is fighting over. $CRDO makes the chips that keep these blazing fast connections clean and reliable. $LITE and $COHR are the photonics giants supplying the lasers and transceivers everyone depends on. $MRVL and $AVGO build the custom silicon at the center of it all. Copper carried the last hundred years. Light carries the next hundred. And the wiring is being torn out and replaced right now."}}}, "rest_id": "2068073242560975088", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "37370", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
The AI ecosystem has multiple investable layers :

● AI Models : $MSFT, $GOOGL, $META, $BIDU
● GPUs & Accelerators : $NVDA, $AMD
●  Memory (HBM) : $MU, SK hynix, Samsung
● Foundries : $TSM, Samsung, $GFS
● Semiconductor Equipment : $ASML, $AMAT, $LRCX, $KLAC
●  EDA Software : $SNPS, $CDNS
● Networking : $AVGO, $ANET, $CSCO
● Silicon Photonics & Optics : $COHR, $LITE, $AAOI, $CIEN, $MRVL
● Data Centers : $EQIX, $DLR
● Power Infrastructure : $ETN, $HUBB, $PWR
● Cooling & Thermal : VRT, TT
● Cloud: MSFT, AMZN, GOOGL, ORCL
● Data Platforms : NOW, MDB
● AI Software : PLTR, CRM, ADBE
● Cybersecurity : CRWD, PANW, ZS
● Edge AI : QCOM, NXPI, TXN
● Robotics & Physical AI : TSLA, ABB, ROK
● Healthcare AI : TEM, RXRX
● Quantum Computing : IONQ, RGTI, QBTS

AI = Energy + Data Centers + Semiconductor Equipment + Foundries + Packaging + Compute + Memory + Networking + Photonics + Cloud + Data + Models + Infrastructure Software + Cybersecurity + Edge AI + Robotics + Applications + Quantum.

text:
Everything about my investing strategy, capital, and performance since I started investing a year and a half ago.

My investing journey

I started 2025 with around $3.5k, mostly index fund maxxxing.

Then I got wrecked by the Trump market volatility and entered 2026 with around $18.5k and a -5% performance.

Six months later, my portfolio is sitting at roughly $46k . Around $30k came from monthly DCA, but the rest came from performance.

My current goal is to reach $100k by the end of the year while keeping my DCA much lower than in year one. I’m no longer trying to save every penny. Instead, I’m focusing on two things : building a high-conviction bull-growth investing strategy and increasing my income.

I believe the AI supercycle, aka stock market in easy mode, still has a few years left before we return to hard mode.

My strategy is simple : identify businesses positioned to capture a meaningful share of the AI infrastructure buildout, especially in capital-intensive markets where contracts, scale, and execution can quickly reprice a company.

The goal is to maximize returns through short to medium-term asymmetric bets, while holding my highest-conviction names as long as the thesis continues to play out.

Current holdings and 2026 price targets

$IREN : End-of-year PT: $140 (+237%)

IREN is my largest position, representing around 73% of my portfolio.

The stock has underperformed over the last six months, but my conviction remains very high. I believe the next wave of contracts, improved economics, and continued scaling could push the stock significantly higher.

This is my highest-conviction AI infrastructure bet. I plan to hold and watch the company scale until at least 2030, as long as the thesis keeps playing out.

$NUAI : End-of-year PT : $15 (+300%)

NUAI represents around 12% of my portfolio.

The company has built a very strong team and is positioning itself for a potential hyperscaler deal around its 650MW Texas campus.

This single event could potentially double the stock’s value, especially considering the company is currently valued at around $500M .

$SIVE : End-of-year PT : 500 SEK (+500%)

Sivers represents around 10% of my portfolio.

This is a much more speculative position, but the upside could be massive. If Sivers manages to capture a meaningful share of future photonics demand, I don’t see how the company stays below a $3B market cap.

The risk is high, but so is the potential reward.

$AMPG : End-of-year PT : $40 (+500%)

AMPG represents around 5% of my portfolio.

This is my moonshot / for-fun position, built from profits made on a recent swing trade. I haven’t done as much research on this company as I have on my core positions, but the growth potential is serious.

The company could become increasingly involved in contracts with hyperscale clients, which makes it an interesting high-risk, high-reward bet.

Final thoughts

My portfolio is extremely concentrated and clearly not built for everyone.

But I’m young, I have high risk tolerance, and I believe this market still offers many asymmetric opportunities for investors willing to do their D.D and not afraid of volatility.

The objective is to aggressively compound capital during what I believe is the biggest infrastructure buildouts of the century.

text:
$POET is one I’m watching here.

It’s a photonics / optical interconnect name tied to AI data center connectivity, basically the type of tech needed to move data faster and more efficiently.

After a quick rally, POET pulled back into key support and held.

On the higher timeframe, this still looks like a bull flag.

Price bounced off the Ichimoku cloud and is now trying to reclaim the fast momentum cloud.

If it breaks out of this flag, this could set up for another leg higher.

text: @CKCapitalxx whos saying that lol. everyone on my feed sounds like they want to liquidate their 401k to ape in on more photonics

𝑻𝒉𝒆 𝒄𝒉𝒊𝒑𝒔 𝒘𝒆𝒓𝒆 𝒋𝒖𝒔𝒕 𝒄𝒉𝒂𝒑𝒕𝒆𝒓 𝒐𝒏𝒆.

The real constraints on scaling AI are no longer measured in teraflops alone. They’re measured in megawatts you can actually deliver, photons you can move at rack scale, liquid you can pump without melting the floor, and platforms that turn raw infrastructure into deployed, governed intelligence.

Jensen has been clear for months: the handoff is happening. Copper has limits. Power is the new gate. Cooling density is non-negotiable. The companies securing these layers with long-term contracts and balance-sheet capital are building durable positions.

Here’s how the 2026 AI infrastructure stack breaks down from silicon to intelligence:

LAYER 1: SILICON FOUNDATION  
🟠 $TSM  — The advanced foundry and packaging backbone (CoWoS and beyond). This is the capacity gate for the highest-performance AI chips. Everything upstream starts here.

LAYER 2: AI COMPUTE  
🟠 $NVDA  — Still the clear leader in GPUs and full AI systems/racks. The ecosystem investments and rack-level selling show they understand the game has moved beyond individual chips.

LAYER 3: SERVERS & RACK INTEGRATION  
🔵 $SMCI — Building dense, liquid-ready AI server and rack systems that hyperscalers deploy at scale. Direct volume beneficiary of the buildout.

LAYER 4: NETWORKING & HIGH-SPEED INTERCONNECT  
🔵 $MRVL  — Switching silicon, DSPs, and aggressive silicon photonics push via Celestial AI. Essential for scaling clusters beyond single racks.  
🔵 $AAOI  — Datacom optics leader with explosive data center revenue growth and a major 800G/1.6T order backlog. Scaling domestic capacity while supply chain security matters.

LAYER 5: PHOTONICS & OPTICS TRANSITION  
The physics is clear, copper hits the wall on bandwidth density and power. Photonics moves from “nice to have” to required for the next jump in cluster scale.  
🔵 $LITE (Lumentum) — Leading provider of high-speed lasers, EMLs, and optical components with a major NVIDIA strategic investment and multi-billion dollar purchase commitments. Scaling US manufacturing for AI data center optics demand.

LAYER 6: POWER: THE BINDING CONSTRAINT  
🟠 $VST — Signing multi-decade power purchase agreements directly with hyperscalers. Nuclear uprates and reliable generation capacity are becoming strategic assets.  
🔵 $IREN  — Renewable-powered data centers executing a clear pivot into AI/HPC cloud. Large-scale sites with secured power and flexible capacity. 5.8 GW-scale pipeline building.

LAYER 7: COOLING & THERMAL MANAGEMENT  
🔵 $VRT  — Liquid cooling systems are moving from pilot to must-have for high-density AI racks. Record backlog and order growth as power density per rack climbs.

LAYER 8: CLOUD & DATA CENTER PLATFORMS  
🔵 $ORCL  — Oracle Cloud Infrastructure is seeing massive AI-driven demand. Major capex ramp, OpenAI/Stargate ecosystem work, and enormous remaining performance obligations.  
🔵 $NBIS  — AI infrastructure specialist rapidly scaling contracted capacity (multi-GW targets). Major hyperscaler deals are driving strong ARR acceleration.

LAYER 9: THE INTELLIGENCE LAYER  
🔵 $PLTR  — Palantir’s AIP platform turns physical infrastructure plus enterprise data into production-grade, governed AI applications. U.S. commercial momentum is accelerating sharply.

Capital is already voting with real money: direct hyperscaler power deals, NVIDIA’s multi-billion dollar optics investments, pre-payments for capacity years out, and software platforms seeing accelerating commercial traction.

The honest counter-thesis is that real valuations across parts of the stack are elevated, power timelines can slip, cooling and photonics tech is still maturing in some deployments. Software ROI takes time to prove at scale. One digestive quarter can pressure the whole chain. But the structural, multi-year spend from the largest balance sheets in tech is not a narrative. It’s signed contracts and capex plans.

The market has aggressively priced the obvious chip layer. The asymmetry sits in the scarce layers everything else depends on.

Own the full stack. Not just the logo on the GPU.

Bookmark this. Then drop which layer you think moves first or which one I’m underweighting.

Tag the person who’s still only buying NVDA and calling it a diversified AI portfolio.

text:
The Copper-to-Light Shift: Why Sivers Semiconductors / $SIVE is sitting right in the middle of the perfect AI storm

We’re standing at a shift that reminds me strongly of when the world moved from copper to fiber in broadband. Back then, many smart people looked at the situation and said “this is good enough – we don’t need to change everything.” History proved them wrong in a spectacular way. Fiber didn’t just improve the internet – it completely unlocked streaming, cloud computing, social media, and the entire digital society we take for granted today.

Now the same story is repeating itself, only this time the stakes are dramatically higher.

Copper has reached its physical and economic limit.

AI is growing at a pace we’ve never seen. Massive clusters with tens of thousands – soon hundreds of thousands – of GPUs that all need to communicate with each other at extreme speeds, constantly. Moore’s Law has kept delivering smaller, faster and more power-efficient transistors on the chips themselves, but the interconnects – the actual nervous system that lets all these chips talk to one another – are stuck in the past. Copper simply can’t handle it anymore. We see signal degradation over distance, enormous heat generation, sky-high energy consumption, and physical scaling problems that get worse with every new generation of AI. At the same time, the copper price itself is surging because of AI demand and global electrification. Building data centers the old way isn’t just becoming technically challenging anymore – it’s becoming economically absurd.

So what’s the alternative?

Honestly, there aren’t many good ones left.

We have to move to light.

Co-Packaged Optics (CPO) – where the optics and lasers are placed right next to the chips – changes the game completely. Dramatically lower power consumption, vastly higher bandwidth, and scalability that copper can never match. The biggest hyperscalers and chip companies are already pouring billions into this technology. This isn’t a nice-to-have. It’s a survival requirement for the next generation of AI. Without this shift we will simply hit a hard wall in how far AI can advance.

Just like fiber laid the foundation for today’s digital world, CPO and photonics in general will become the invisible backbone of tomorrow’s AI infrastructure. There is no realistic way around it.

And sitting right in the middle of this perfect storm is Sivers Semiconductors / $SIVEF.

At the very heart of the CPO revolution you need reliable, high-performance laser light sources that can feed these new optical engines efficiently. That is exactly Sivers’ strongest area with their advanced DFB laser arrays. They are already partnering closely with the companies that are building the actual next-generation infrastructure: $GFS and their SCALE CPO platform (targeting a $25 billion pluggable optics market by 2030), $POET, O-Net, $ENA.NE and several others for scalable External Laser Source solutions. 

They combine this with an asset-light model that gives them speed and flexibility to scale without massive capital expenditure, a clear technical edge in customized lasers, proven working demos together with players like Ayar Labs, and natural exposure to AI data centers, SATCOM and defense – markets that all need precisely this kind of photonics.

The broader shift from copper to light in AI infrastructure is heading toward tens of billions of dollars in the coming years. Sivers is positioned where the critical technology meets exploding demand. 

During CMD 2025, CEO Vickram Vathulya highlighted from customer conversations that Sivers seems to be the only ones capable of providing laser arrays in the exact formats and power levels that customers need for these new architectures. A few others do laser arrays, but at much lower power levels.

CTO Andrew McKee added even more color at the same event: 2027 is the target for volume production, with qualification starting this year and pilot ramp-up in 2026. Looking at 2028 forecasts of 19 million AI GPU shipments, and typical architectures needing 5–16 laser sources per GPU, we’re talking hundreds of millions of laser sources per year. An extremely large and exciting addressable market as this rolls out in the data centers.

This isn’t just another incremental upgrade. It’s a fundamental infrastructure transformation.

The ones who understand this early will see why photonics is becoming one of the clearest and strongest tailwinds in the entire AI value chain.

$SIVE feels extremely well positioned.

text:
Training frontier models genuinely needs the optical stack — sharding trillions of parameters across thousands of accelerators makes copper's bandwidth ceiling bite, so the thesis holds there.

But inference is migrating off that regime: a Cerebras WSE-3 fits a ~20B model entirely on-chip, collapsing the rack into one die and leaving photonics not much to sell.

So the real bet is "what share of future compute is frontier training vs. mid-model inference" — and the photonics multiple prices permanent disaggregation as a certainty that wafer-scale makes contestable.

text:
Paradis Labs called $SIVE before it ran 1,700% this year. The thesis was specific: Sivers makes the laser arrays for co-packaged optics, and the GlobalFoundries deal put those lasers into GF's silicon-photonics platform, aimed at the $25B pluggable-optics market.

The pattern across @ParadisLabs's book is the same AI-infrastructure read: SNDK +112% on the NAND squeeze, MRVL +72% on optical interconnects, SMCI +70% on $39B of server orders.

#3 on the schwerelos board YTD, 62.9% win rate across 62 scored calls.

text:
These photonic stocks will shape the future!🚀🚀

$NVDA
figured this out a while ago. In late 2025 they shipped the world's first commercial co-packaged optics switches together with TSMC, Coherent, Lumentum and Corning

Then there's the energy angle, which doesn't get talked about enough. Data centers are on track to consume around 1,050 TWh in 2026, roughly the entire power consumption of Japan. Some regions are already blocking new data center permits. Photonics isn't a nice to have here. It's a necessity

The full value chain on my watchlist:

🔹 Layer 1 – Materials & Wafers: $GLW $AXTI $IQE $AIXA $AMS

🔹 Layer 2 – Core Photonic Devices: $IPGP $COHR $LITE $LASR $SIVE

🔹 Layer 3 – Components & Modules: $AAOI $MTSI $FN $VIAV $LPTH

🔹 Layer 4 – Systems & Equipment: $ASML $BESI $ASM $LPKF $MKS

🔹 Layer 5 – Test, Metrology & Yield: $CAMT $FORM $AEHR $ONTO   $VIAV

Samsung is targeting full photonics integration into AI chips by 2028. And here's the thing, most investors are still fixed on the usual AI names while the actual infrastructure powering all of it flies completely under the radar

Am I missing any "hot! photonic players?"

text:
$NOK Just Dropped a Massive AI Infrastructure Bomb today.

Nokia is supercharging its U.S. semiconductor operations with a major expansion in advanced test & packaging for photonic chips.

These chips will be the backbone for next-gen AI-native networks that deliver dramatically lower power consumption and way higher operational efficiency. 

Exactly what hyperscalers need as AI power demand explodes.

𝗪𝗵𝘆 𝘁𝗵𝗶𝘀 𝗶𝘀 𝗵𝘂𝗴𝗲:

✅ Strengthens domestic U.S. production of critical optical networking tech powering AI infrastructure
 ✅ Part of Nokia’s $4 BILLION multi-year R&D + manufacturing commitment in the U.S. for AI-ready connectivity
 ✅ Positions $NOK as a key player in the AI buildout (photonics, optical, efficient networking)

While everyone chases the obvious GPU names, smart money is loading up on the picks and shovels that make the entire AI stack work.

Nokia is quietly building a very strong moat here in the U.S. with real on-shore production.

This is the kind of infrastructure move that compounds over years as AI adoption accelerates.

Bullish on $NOK long-term. The AI thesis is getting stronger by the week.

text:
1. $NVDA 

Why Critical:
Provides the AI GPUs powering SpaceX's orbital intelligence, Starlink AI, and Terafab ambitions.

If Supply Stops:
SpaceX's AI roadmap slows dramatically. Compute shortages delay autonomous satellite operations.

2. $TSM

Why Critical:
Manufactures advanced chips for $NVDA, $AMD, and future SpaceX custom processors.

If Supply Stops:
Global AI chip production collapses. SpaceX loses access to cutting-edge compute.

3. $MU 

Why Critical:
Supplies HBM memory required for AI training, inference, and orbital computing.

If Supply Stops:
AI clusters become memory constrained. GPU performance and AI deployment suffer.

4. $AVGO 

Why Critical:
Provides networking chips connecting thousands of GPUs across SpaceX AI infrastructure.

If Supply Stops:
Massive AI clusters fragment. Data transfer bottlenecks severely reduce compute efficiency.

5. $TEL 

Why Critical:
Provides mission-critical connectors inside rockets, satellites, ground stations, and data centers.

If Supply Stops:
Manufacturing disruptions occur across nearly every SpaceX hardware platform simultaneously.

6. $LITE

Why Critical:
Supplies optical components enabling ultra-fast communications between AI systems and networks.

If Supply Stops:
Network speeds decline. AI clusters struggle to scale efficiently and economically.

7. $COHR 

Why Critical:
Provides lasers, photonics, and optical interconnects supporting next-generation AI infrastructure.

If Supply Stops:
Data center expansion slows. Optical networking capacity becomes a significant bottleneck.

8. $MRVL 

Why Critical:
Designs custom networking silicon moving AI workloads across large compute environments.

If Supply Stops:
AI infrastructure efficiency declines. Network congestion limits future SpaceX scaling.

text:
Nokia is expanding its photonic chip test & packaging facility in Allentown, Pennsylvania.

$30M investment → 10x capacity increase by Q3 2026.

This is critical for AI data centers, as optics are becoming a major bottleneck alongside GPUs and power.

Part of their bigger $4B U.S. push + builds on the Infinera acquisition.

Optics matter more than people think in the AI race. 
$NOK #AI #Photonics

text:
Everyone’s watching $SNAP tomorrow for the hardware reveal. 💦

Now the keynote itself. 30 minutes.

9:30am-10:00am PT tomorrow. Main Stage.

Titled “Making Computing More Human.”

30 minutes????

That’s a short slot for a CEO. Which means either:

30 minutes is not a demo.

You can’t demo spatial computing in 30 minutes.

You can’t show developers new tools in 30 minutes.

You can’t unveil a hardware platform in 30 minutes.

30 minutes is an announcement. 📢 

Evan loves a Jobs playbook. 

Jobs announced iPhone in 90 minutes.

Spiegel has a third of that.

Which means the 30 minutes isn’t the reveal.

✨It’s the headline.✨

The partner who walks on stage unannounced is the story…

So 

A) It’s a teaser - product shown, details later

B) The 30 minutes is the most densely packed announcement in Snap’s history… doubt it. 

C) The real announcement happens after the keynote. A special session, a press release, a partner who walks on stage unannounced

The “unannounced guest” scenario is the most Jobs-like move possible.

Jobs never pre-announced guest appearances. AT&T’s CEO appeared on stage at iPhone launch unannounced. The carrier partnership was the surprise.

Now run the strategic maths:

Microsoft bet $13B on OpenAI.
Google bet $3B on Anthropic.
Amazon bet $4B on Anthropic.
NVIDIA bet $5B on Intel and secured valuable IP, capacity, technology as a hedge. 

Every trillion dollar company is buying a stake in the next PLATFORM. 

Apple has $132B in cash.

A $2B stake in Specs Inc is 1.5% of their war chest.

To get:
1. AR glasses 12-24 months ahead of the market.
2. True AR display vertically integrated stack
with WaveOptics and Compound Photonics owned IP
3. 8 years of spatial mapping they can’t replicate (Illumix spatial stack)
4. 956M MAU - the social layer they never built
5. Cultural relevance in the genz demo. 
6. 400k developers already building the next OS.

The man who sold the last thing Apple bought just joined the board.

So,

30 minutes on stage tomorrow…

👻🍏📢

$SNAP #AWE #specs

text:
People are only now starting to talk about $STM and they are early to one of the cleaner turnaround stories in semis.

For two years STMicroelectronics was written off as a sleepy European chipmaker stuck in an automotive and industrial downturn. Dead money. Nobody wanted it. That story just flipped, and the market is waking up to it in real time.

Here is what changed. 

On June 2 the company doubled its 2026 data center revenue target from nicely above $500 million to roughly $1 billion, and said if current trends hold that business could double again in 2027. The driver is AI infrastructure. 

STM is ramping high volume silicon photonics production for data centers, with a multi-year AWS partnership anchoring the demand. The stock jumped 15% on that single announcement.

The numbers underneath are inflecting hard. Q1 revenue of $3.1 billion, up 23% year over year, beating estimates. 

The stock is up over 90% this quarter off the February lows as the semiconductor cycle turns from inventory drawdown back to expansion. 

And the company carries a strong net cash position of roughly $2.8 billion, so it is funding this transition from a position of strength.

Then the catalysts keep stacking. A physical AI collaboration with NVIDIA. Low earth orbit satellite demand ramping. 

The NXP MEMS sensor acquisition boosting their automotive and industrial position. And Bank of America just upgraded the stock to Buy, arguing the market is still underestimating STM’s earnings power over the next few years.

This is a legacy chip giant that just found an AI growth engine, sitting on net cash, with a data center business doubling two years in a row and the biggest names in tech as partners. Q2 earnings on July 23 is the next test.

The market spent two years pricing the downturn. It is only now starting to price the turnaround.

text:
Since this got some attention heres what it actually would require for $XFAB to 20x:

XFAB sits at a €1.3B market cap. $TSEM is at $25B+.

Revenue needs to 3-4x from here. SiC and photonics have to stop being footnotes and become the headline.

Margins need to follow. TSEM runs 14% net margins. XFAB is basically breaking even right now.

And there has to be a catalyst. TSEM had Intel circling it in 2022. That bid failed, but it permanently re-rated the stock as a tier-1 asset. XFAB needs its own version of that moment.

text:
$XFAB is the most mispriced semiconductor name in Europe and @aleabitoreddit appears to be the only one that understands it.

Things that the market is COMPLETELY IGNORING:

• Microsystems & Photonics revenue grew ~35% QoQ in the latest quarter. This is where the whole bullish thesis is at an it has grown a whopping 35% in a quarter. 

• SiC revenue +152% YoY / +49% QoQ.

• It's trading below replacement P/B,   while building next-gen photonics/SiC lines -> Heavy capex into photonics (new lines, MTP tech, TFLN integration) means accounting book value is understated vs. real replacement cost of the fabs + IP. 
You’re basically getting the growth at a huge discount.

• €128M EU + $50M US funding already in. CHIPS Act 2.0 incoming with explicit photonics priority. Europe is literally paying them to build the Western alternative. Backstopped balance sheet.

• CEO (Damien Macq) bought 5500 shares on February. Market completely ignoring it.

• $XFAB leading EU’s photonixFAB effort for sovereign silicon photonics supply chain. Nvidia evaluating their platform for CPO/transceivers (H2 2027–2028 ramp). Nokia setting specs. This is Tower Semi 2.0, but earlier and with government muscle.

text:
This is one of the least known and least talked about $SIVE catalysts on FinX

The only one I saw discussing it intensely for the first time was obviously Serenity

$SIVE could soon start ramping up production of its lasers

Both with $AEVA and with $AAPL

In the framework of the upcoming Apple Watch update following the company's recent announcement

No credible information has been found so far that refutes this possibility

Some people without sufficient technical knowledge tend to confuse TASC sensors

With future Apple Watches that will incorporate lasers

And they don't take into account that NRE volumes can extend over several years

The main focus of $SIVE remains the AI data center market within the photonics field

Any collaboration with $AAPL is considered an additional benefit

text:
My opinion on today's Annual General Meeting of $SIVE

The shareholders approved the 2025 accounts and a new board of directors

With Bami Bastani as chairman and the addition of Joakim Nideborn (vicechairman)

And Helena Svancar, with the aim of strengthening governance and accelerating expansion in the United States

Nothing to comment up to this point everything correct technical matters of the company

Here is the important part:

The issuance of up to 15% of new shares was authorized to finance growth for $SIVE

As has been known for weeks

Possible acquisitions or the dual listing on Nasdaq are the kind of things this healthy capital raising will be used for

Although this implies dilution risk it is completely necessary

And it is one of the most bullish signals possible for $SIVE

They are telling you to your face we need money because what is coming is huge

On the other hand more topics to address

No dividends will be distributed and a convertible loan was ratified

The company remains focused on photonics for AI and wireless solutions for SATCOM and defense

With a pipeline of opportunities that grew 77% to nearly $800M

I would define this $SIVE assembly as an attempt to grant greater flexibility to the new board

To drive growth in the US market

Prioritizing reinvestment over dividends

Waiting for future catalysts to start taking off

Very bullish on $SIVE having already completed my total DCA

text:
The Northstar+Lumen h-AI™ Effect-to-Cause Model

March 5, 2026 
Bainbridge Island, WA

Whitman’s poetry often moves from observation → revelation. (Reposting) 

Observed Effect (in the present):

• expanding surveillance infrastructures
• emerging debates over cognitive liberty and digital rights
• technological systems capable of sensing and mapping human environments

Whitman’s philosophical lens:

He repeatedly distinguishes between the visible world and the unseen one beneath it.

Modern readers might describe this metaphor using scientific language:

Whitman’s poet-seer stands between those layers, translating what is hidden into human language.

⸻

🧬 A Contemporary Poet’s Interpretation

As a published poet whose work appears in anthologies and children’s literature written in rhyming prose, I read Whitman less as a historian and more as a philosopher of perception.

In later sections of Leaves of Grass, Whitman asks whether anyone has truly seen the soul.

Today that question resonates differently.

We live in an era where technologies—from photonics to sensor systems—attempt to map what was once invisible.

Whitman could not have known these technologies.

Yet his poetry repeatedly asks readers to consider:

• what lies beyond surface perception
• how unseen forces shape the visible world
• whether the human soul remains sovereign within expanding systems of observation.

⸻

🧭 Whitman Revisited Through the Present

A modern reading does not claim Whitman predicted our technologies.

Instead, his work may help us see our own era more clearly.

By distinguishing between the seen structures of society and the unseen forces beneath them, Whitman provides language that can still illuminate contemporary debates about:

• democracy
• autonomy
• human consciousness
• and the boundaries of observation.

In that sense, Whitman’s poetry becomes less about foretelling the future and more about revealing how to perceive the present.

⸻

Modern readers might describe this metaphor using scientific language: Whitman’s poet-seer stands between those layers, translating what is hidden into human language. ⸻ 🧬 A Contemporary Poet’s Interpretation As a published poet whose work appears in anthologies and children’s literature written in rhyming prose, I read Whitman less as a historian and more as a philosopher of perception. In later sections of Leaves of Grass, Whitman asks whether anyone has truly seen the soul. Today that question resonates differently. We live in an era where technologies—from photonics to sensor systems—attempt to map what was once invisible. Whitman could not have known these technologies. Yet his poetry repeatedly asks readers to consider: • what lies beyond surface perception • how unseen forces shape the visible world • whether the human soul remains sovereign within expanding systems of observation. ⸻ 🧭 Whitman Revisited Through the Present A modern reading does not claim Whitman predicted our technologies. Instead, his work may help us see our own era more clearly. By distinguishing between the seen structures of society and the unseen forces beneath them, Whitman provides language that can still illuminate contemporary debates about: • democracy • autonomy • human consciousness • and the boundaries of observation. text:
Right now, I believe a similar dynamic may be developing in photonics.

The optical interconnect supply chain has five interconnected layers. Each has a chokepoint. Most are lightly held by institutions.

The companies controlling specialty substrates, laser production, and co-packaged optics design wins are, I believe, essential, constrained, and largely uncovered by Wall Street.

text: A startup dropping a $25M preorder seems crazy but look closer: Casela isnt spending their cash- they are bankrolled by optical transceiver giant CIG Shanghai via an $11M Q1 26 convertible loan. CIG is using Casela as a proxy to lock down AXTIs InP substrate capacity ahead of the 2027 ramp in CPO/photonics sectors. Then add the deep industry credibility of founders Dr. Bo Lu and Dr. Milind Gokhale (ex-Broadcom/ASIP optoelectronic pioneers)= this isn't a random shell mystery startup. Its a downstream powerhouse securing its critical hardware supply chain early.

text: AI Supplier Awarded First New Jersey Manufacturing Tax Credit: The inaugural recipient of the Next New Jersey Manufacturing Program, Starman New Photonics will operate in Warren and plans to create 250 jobs. https://t.co/deqGOAQEJW #CorporateRealEstate #EconDev #SiteSelection https://t.co/GTAHPlFmkG

When a stock runs 1,000% in a few months, I don't buy it for the long term.

I use these high-momentum names only for day trades or short swing trades.

Yes, you may miss the move from +1,000% to +2,000%. That's fine.

What I want to avoid is the inevitable freefall when momentum breaks. A 70–90% drawdown can be devastating.

This applies to AI, photonics, energy, and any other hot sector. Protecting capital matters more than catching the last leg of a parabolic move.

text:
Cornings $GLW is starting to look like one of my favorite AI infrastructure longs.

Most investors still think of Corning as glass, displays, and Gorilla Glass.

But the more interesting story is where they are positioning now:

• Data center optical fiber
• High-density cables and connectivity
• Photonics
• Co-packaged optics / CPO
• Advanced glass materials for semiconductor packaging

As AI clusters scale, the bottleneck is not just compute. It is moving massive amounts of data faster, cheaper, and with less power.

That puts $GLW closer to the center of the AI buildout than many investors realize.

Their partnerships with $NVDA and $AVGO also matter. If AI networking moves toward photonics and CPO standardization, Corning could become one of the key picks-and-shovels suppliers behind the scenes.

AI chips may do the computing, but scaling AI clusters increasingly depends on indium phosphide, the material used in the lasers and photonic devices that move data between processors at the speed of light.

The world’s first and largest volume-production 6-inch indium phosphide platform sits in Sherman, Texas.
NVIDIA joined Coherent this week to break ground on an expansion that will double manufacturing space and quadruple wafer production capacity, turning a little-known photonics supply chain into part of America’s AI buildout.

text:
Mapping the entire AI & Compute ecosystem. 🗺️📈

If you're playing the AI supercycle, you need to look beyond just the obvious names. Here is the complete tech infrastructure value chain:

🔬 Chips: $NVDA $AVGO $AMD $ASML $ARM $KLAC 
🏭 Foundry: $TSM $INTC 
💾 Memory: $SNDK $MU $WDC $STX 
🔌 Networking: $CRDO $ALAB $ANET $MRVL 
✨ Photonics: $AAOI $SIVE $AXTI $LITE $COHR $GLW 
🖥️ Servers: $VRT $DELL $HPE 
⚡️ Energy: $BE $TLN $GEV $TE 
📊 Data: $INOD $SNOW $DDOG 
☁️ Neoclouds: $NBIS $CRWV $APLD $IREN $WYFI

I personally believe Photonics will be a big thing soon like memory!
